summary: The requirement for hinge opening axis transfer is considered compulsory in completely edentulous patients because they lack natural teeth to provide occlusal guidance, necessitating a stable reference point for function and retention. However, this principle applies exclusively to full arch losses; in partially edentulous cases, existing natural teeth allow for self-guided movement, reducing dependence on a transferred hinge axis based on individual case factors.

However, this principle applies exclusively to full arch losses; in partially edentulous cases, existing natural teeth allow for self-guided movement, reducing dependence on a transferred hinge axis based on individual case factors.. Answer choice: B
